10000.event: Born into an ordinary family, your first cry was so loud the midwife praised your lung capacity.

10001.event: Born with a slight smile, the midwife said you had a happy face.
10001.postEvent: That photo was later looked at again and again by your parents.

10002.event: On your first night, you cried nonstop; elders muttered you saw something unclean.
10002.postEvent: At dawn, you fell into a deep sleep, as if nothing had happened.

10003.event: Born weak and sickly, you had a fever right after birth and needed days in an incubator.

10004.event: Your first cry sounded faintly like you were saying 'Mom', which brought tears to your exhausted mother's eyes instantly.

10005.event: On your first birthday, you grabbed the farthest crayon; relatives said you'd become a painter.
10005.postEvent: Actually, you just liked the color.

10006.event: At age one, you wobbled, fell, hit your forehead on a coffee table, leaving a faint scar.

10007.event: Your busy parents sent you to your grandmother's countryside house for six months, where you chased chickens and grew strong.

10008.event: At your birth, the delivery room light flickered; nurses exchanged puzzled looks until your cry restored normalcy.
10008.postEvent: No one noticed this anomaly. In the days that followed, you occasionally giggled at the air.

10009.event: At your birth, a loud drill outside startled you, weakening your cry and causing sleeplessness for days.

10010.event: Your mother read you picture books daily; you stared wide-eyed, tracing colorful pictures despite not understanding.

10011.event: At two and a half, you couldn't speak; they suspected delay, but you were just lazy.

10012.event: Dad got a promotion and raise, so the family moved to a bigger house with your own star-stickered room.

10013.event: At the park sandpit, you dug up a warm white stone that brought indescribable peace.
10013.postEvent: Later, the stone was lost, but you always dreamed of it glowing.

10014.event: On your third birthday, your grandfather had to slap out the candle you couldn't blow out.
10014.postEvent: When the flame went out, the frosting on the cake had melted into a puddle.

10015.event: Crying on your first day of kindergarten, you became best friends with another crying boy.
10015.postEvent: Later, you built blocks together and had a happy day.

10016.event: After a fever where you saw ceiling spots like glowing fish, your vision becomes extraordinarily sharp.
10016.postEvent: After that, you occasionally saw faint patterns in the air, but you never told anyone.

10017.event: Mom bought you a small bicycle with training wheels. You rode it wobbly all afternoon in the neighborhood.
10017.postEvent: Your knees had a few extra band-aids, but you finally could ride without an adult holding you.

10018.event: Suddenly afraid of the dark, you nightly check under the bed and closet, muttering 'go away'.
10018.postEvent: Mom and Dad thought you were just being fussy, but you clearly knew something was indeed there.

10019.event: You secretly ate a whole box of ice cream and rolled in pain from stomachache that night.
10019.postEvent: Your mother scolded you while rubbing your belly, and you cried, promising never to be greedy again.

10020.event: On a kindergarten field trip, you saw vast sunflower fields and chased a white butterfly into them.
10020.postEvent: After coming back, your painting of sunflowers was posted on the wall by the teacher.

10021.event: Your cousin snatched your toy, you fought, and adults pulled you apart.
10021.postEvent: Later, you built a castle out of blocks as a peace offering, but you still felt a bit upset inside.

10022.event: You found a translucent pebble with a silver thread inside; hid it and dreamed of flying as a bird.
10022.postEvent: When you woke up, the stone was gone, but from then on you were no longer afraid of heights.

10023.event: You learned to write your name, scribbled it on paper, and proudly showed your dad.
10023.postEvent: Your dad said your handwriting looked like cockroach crawls, but he specially gave you an extra drumstick for dinner.

10024.event: You were picked for the Little Duck dance, but nerves made you freeze and cry on stage.
10024.postEvent: The teacher had to carry you off stage, and you became famous as the 'crybaby' of the kindergarten.

10025.event: Dad lost his job; you didn't understand, but Mom smiled less and you dared not ask for toys.
10025.postEvent: You learned to play quietly by yourself.

10026.event: You got lost briefly, met a black cat that nodded, and it led you back to your mom.
10026.postEvent: The adults called it a stray cat, but you insisted it was a magical spirit.

10027.event: You lost a front tooth, lisped, and refused to speak all day after the neighbor boy mocked you.
10027.postEvent: Later, Mom slipped you a coin and said the tooth fairy had come. You giggled again.

10028.event: You dug in the sandbox all afternoon and found a plastic dinosaur you thought dinosaurs buried.
10028.postEvent: You carefully washed it and put it into your treasure box.

10029.event: On your birthday, flames turned pale blue and self-extinguished thrice; your wish felt heard.
10029.postEvent: After that, you always felt like something was watching over you, and you acted carefully.

10030.event: Despite nervous sweaty palms, you ranked near the top on your first big exam.
10030.postEvent: Mom happily took you out for ice cream.

10031.event: A few older kids pushed you in the hallway. You gathered your courage and reported it to the homeroom teacher.
10031.postEvent: The homeroom teacher sternly scolded them. Your own courage surprised you.

10032.event: Dad held the seat as you pedaled wobbly, fell when he let go, but got up and rode alone.

10033.event: On your way home, you entered an alley where a three-legged black cat winked and vanished.
10033.postEvent: When you got home, you told your mom, but she said you were seeing things.

10034.event: Alex said he'd move next semester, so after school you cried under the covers.

10035.event: Nervous on stage, spotlight stinging your eyes, you forgot your third line but classmates kindly applauded.

10036.event: Feverish, you felt your mom repeatedly press a wet towel to your forehead as she whispered a prayer.
10036.postEvent: After you recovered, you felt like you had grown up a little.

10037.event: Under a plane tree, you found a bleeding sparrow, wrapped it in your scarf, took it home, and fed it.
10037.postEvent: Two weeks later, the day it flew away, you stood on the balcony for a long time.

10038.event: Under the dome, stars rotated slowly; you learned they were larger than the sun, planting a seed of astronomy.

10039.event: You counted change twice from the corner store lady, then Doraemon comic brightened your weekend.

10041.event: The jump rope hits your ankle; you fall, bleed, but keep jumping as classmates cheer.

10042.event: You copied tadpole-like symbols from yellowed pages, dreamed of an ink-blue sea, and woke with a damp pillowcase.

10043.event: Xiaoqiang provoked you first, but the teacher only saw you turn and made you stand; you stopped listening to him.

10044.event: An attic box holds a one-eyed doll in a faded dress; staring brings a chill, but Mom won't toss it.
10044.postEvent: Later, you cover it with a red cloth before you dare to sleep.

10045.event: You clutch the admission letter, board the green train away from home, with scenery flying past.
10045.postEvent: The September sun is blinding. You squint, feeling that the future is like the distant mountains—hazy but enticing.

10046.event: Late-night dorm talk: roommate brings out homemade chili sauce; you four munch buns and chat until 3 AM.
10046.postEvent: Later, you tasted many gourmet foods, but nothing compares to the flavor of those buns that night.

10047.event: She always sat by the library window, sunlight gilding her hair; you finally passed her a note.
10047.postEvent: She smiled. Your heart skipped a beat. It felt like spring had fully bloomed.

10048.event: He said let's be friends; the soup suddenly tastes bland as you stare at your phone.
10048.postEvent: For the first time, you realized that heartache isn't just a metaphor—it's a real physical sensation in your chest.

10049.event: You stand at a boba shop till your feet swell, but find joy in 30% sweetness.
10049.postEvent: Use your first paycheck to buy mom a silk scarf; she calls it wasteful but smiles warmly.

10050.event: You see a 59 on your calculus grade, hearing your roommate's distant cheers.
10050.postEvent: After running ten laps, you lie on the grass stargazing; failure suddenly feels less bad.

10051.event: As you type frantically at your computer late at night, your story's characters spring to life while an unknown scent drifts in through the window.
10051.postEvent: You rub your eyes; the period looks like a teardrop. A soft sigh—next room or story?

10052.event: In the old school corridor, you glimpse a girl in white; blinking, only dust remains.
10052.postEvent: Classmates call it a residual image; you hide that the girl looked like your deceased aunt when young.

10053.event: Emergency appendectomy. When you wake from anesthesia, the fluorescent ceiling light is blinding. You suddenly realize how precious health is.
10053.postEvent: On discharge day, the sun is perfect; you breathe air smelling of disinfectant and gardenias.

10054.event: Over winter break, you find your father silently smoking and your mother uneasy; at dinner he says he was laid.
10054.postEvent: You can't sleep that night, figuring out how to take on an extra part-time job next semester.

10055.event: Your advisor calls your name. You go up to receive the award.
10055.postEvent: You send half the prize money home and buy the art supplies you've been eyeing with the other half.

10056.event: You bump into childhood friend Ming at the store; he sheepishly grins in his delivery uniform.
10056.postEvent: You drink soda on the roadside, reminiscing, realizing your worlds are now mountains and seas apart.

10057.event: You skip three days of class for an esports tournament. Your favorite team loses, and you cry like a fool.
10057.postEvent: Your advisor calls you in; staring at his cactus, you realize it's time to grow up.

10058.event: Dreaming on exam eve of a figure's hint, you find a notebook at row three for the destined one.
10058.postEvent: Reviewing the notebook helps you on the exam, but the owner says it was never there.

10059.event: You buy an ancient jade pendant, wear it daily, weaken, collapse, and never wake up.
10059.postEvent: Doctors find no cause; your roommate recalls your whisper "She's come for me," then the jade vanishes.

10060.event: On your {age}th birthday, as you made a wish over the candles, the flames suddenly shot up half a foot.
10060.postEvent: You secretly wrote it in your diary and never mentioned it to anyone.

10061.event: Your first period at school stained your pants, some boys jeered, and you ran home crying, dreading school for days.

10062.event: You cut class with a friend for the arcade, got caught, and earned a three-day suspension plus a school reprimand.

10063.event: At age {age}, your parents slept apart; you acted normal at dinner but heard hushed arguments at night.

10064.event: Late at night, your mirror reflection smiled. You screamed, turned on the light, and hugged a lamp until dawn.

10065.event: Buying a Qi cultivation book improved your running, but nightmares of someone stealing it followed.

10066.event: Pushed to sing at the school anniversary, you stunned the audience and received love letters that night.

10067.event: You found a wet kitten on the way home, hid it under your bed, and fed it milk every day.

10068.event: Your award-winning essay described night sighs between buildings; judges called it imagination.
10068.postEvent: Only you knew that you had really heard it.

10069.event: You dig up a green-glowing stone from a construction sandpile, letting you anticipate the teacher's words by a second, but only when it's with you.
10069.postEvent: You strung it into a necklace and wore it next to your skin, never daring to tell anyone.

10070.event: A physical exam found a 20-degree spinal curve; you wore a plastic brace, got heat rash, and skipped PE.

10071.event: Your best friend exaggerated your complaints, shared them with the class, and you trembled seeing the chat logs.

10072.event: Mom found your love letter; parents lectured that puppy love hurts studies. You felt mortified.

10073.event: During summer vacation, you worked at a bubble tea shop, standing for eight hours until your legs swelled.

10074.event: You left three big math problems blank, got a 58, and slapped yourself in a toilet stall.

10075.event: Frozen shoulder pains you hanging laundry, but touching your granddaughter's uniform makes it worth it.

10076.event: At a pre-retirement party for seniors, blowing out candles showed green smoke like childhood spiritual light.
10076.postEvent: No one noticed. Only you stared at that smoke for a long time.

10077.event: Your physical exam report had three arrows pointing up. The doctor repeatedly urged you to eat less oil and salt.

10078.event: Your daughter called you resilient in a note, so you laughed, cursed, then overwatered the succulent.

10079.event: You wake to find your spouse on the balcony staring at a wilted orchid, claiming it moved, but only shadows.
10079.postEvent: You draped a coat over her and said nothing.

10080.event: The new guy called you boss, his draft flawed but he seemed proud, so you set down the red pen.

10081.event: Old friends' reunion became a medical records exchange until mention of a chapel light; laughter drowned the rain.

10082.event: While renovating the old house, a rusty copper coin fell from a crack in the wall.

10083.event: After finishing your first square dance, stepping on your partner thrice made you feel younger.

10084.event: Your dog of ten years passed away. You buried it under the ginkgo tree downstairs.

10085.event: Writing your will, you added an untouched warm jade pendant from a dark alley for your son.

10086.event: You fainted during a checkup, saw threads from heads, then woke to a nurse feeding you sugar water.

10087.event: While teaching your granddaughter to read in the park, you dismissed the old spirit-glyph as superstition.

10088.event: You didn't expose her when your first love called, paused, and said "wrong number", then added extra salt while cooking.

10089.event: A sudden heart pang made me collapse soundless; I last saw a painted ceiling stain spreading like an eye.

10090.event: After your first offer, you wrote 42 for Soul Number and HR smiled and crossed it out.
10090.postEvent: You never saw that form again.

10091.event: In your second month with a roommate, you found fridge magnets spelling your childhood nickname.
10091.postEvent: You asked your roommate, but he looked clueless.

10092.event: At the reunion, you met your old flame and talked late, avoiding talk of your present lives.
10092.postEvent: After going home, you deleted the contact you just added.

10093.event: An abnormal physical led to three retests; the doctor deemed the machine faulty, then all was normal.

10094.event: Third week of overtime, left office at 2am. Vending machine lights flashed three times in Morse.
10094.postEvent: You bought a can of coffee on a whim and never stayed up that late again.

10095.event: You taught yourself UI design in six months, then your first client tipped you an extra 20%.

10096.event: While father hospitalized, you visited daily. Saw transparent bird on fire hydrant chirping like a boiling kettle.
10096.postEvent: Your father's fever broke the next day.

10097.event: Bought old radio at flea market. At night it turns on, voice says 'March 15, 1987, cloudy, 17 missing.'
10097.postEvent: You checked the newspapers from that year, and indeed 17 people had gone missing in the city that day.

10098.event: After breakup, ate takeout for a month, gained weight, saw thinner reflection encouraging you.
10098.postEvent: Half a year later, you finished a half marathon.

10099.event: All fish in the aquarium swam straight down on your blind date; you froze, then laughed.
10099.postEvent: The next month, you officially started dating.

10100.event: An old man on the bus predicted a building fire; the news later confirmed it.
10100.postEvent: You tried to find that old man again, but couldn't recall his face.

10101.event: After being laid off, you found a lottery ticket in your ex's old journal and won $300.
10101.postEvent: Just enough to cover next quarter's rent.

10102.event: After three all-nighters, your computer crashed before the deadline, but the email had sent while you slept.
10102.postEvent: The attached proposal had a perfect summary you had no memory of writing.

10103.event: Your coffee shop struggled until a customer paid with an ancient coin and vanished. Then long lines formed.

10104.event: After late work, street lights died behind you. Your shadow waved. You stopped breathing; found next day.
10104.postEvent: The next day, your colleague found you dead under a streetlight.

10105.event: First retirement day, you woke early with no plans, watched old men play chess, and lost despite a handicap.

10106.event: Your checkup showed mild hypertension; doctor said cut pork belly, so you hid the report and ordered extra veggies.

10107.event: Your grandchild called you Grandpa on video; you smiled, then rubbed sore knees, feeling old.

10108.event: Finding a photo of your young self, you smiled at your now sparse white hair.

10109.event: At a farmers' market, an old coworker called you 'Old Chen,' and you shared tea, laughing about cafeteria egg thefts.

10110.event: At an old friend's funeral, coffin face didn't match your memory. You laid flowers and turned away, harsh sunlight.
10110.postEvent: When you got home, you started writing a will. The pen scratched across the paper.

10111.event: You joined a senior center calligraphy class. Your shaky 'Eternal' bloomed like a dark cloud, but the teacher praised you.

10112.event: You woke to chopping sounds, checked the kitchen—empty, but moonlight glinted on the knife.

10113.event: You found a stray cat that followed you home and now demands daily food, making you feel useful.

10114.event: A heart attack felled you; in your last moment, the family portrait's subjects all turned to look at you.

10115.event: After a three-day cold war over old newspapers, your spouse bought you braised pork trotters; you grumbled but ate three.

10116.event: Mortgage paid off, you and spouse went for hotpot. She said, 'We spend on ourselves.' You gave her tripe.

10117.event: Your three-year-old pear tree bore rock-hard fruit; its sourness reminded you of one stolen at sixteen.

10118.event: Hospitalized with a cold, you watched a nurse console a coughing old man, recalling nights caring for your feverish child.

10119.event: You found an old manual on energy practices, meditated, felt warmth, then saw sycamore leaves falling.

10120.event: After days of late nights, you had a nosebleed and wondered if it was worth it.

10121.event: You saw a flirty text on your partner's phone, believed their excuse, but doubt lingered.
10121.postEvent: You got fit but still couldn't stop checking your partner's phone at night.

10122.event: Your daughter's first grip makes all the hardship worth it, but at 3 AM you're so sleepy you almost pour formula into the coffee maker.

10123.event: HR fired you Monday morning; your pothos was healthy, but you left first.
10123.postEvent: Unemployed now, your resume went nowhere. You started to understand the people fishing in the park during the day.

10124.event: After a lung scare turned out to be harmless, you celebrated with a family dinner.

10125.event: You secretly financed a motorcycle, rode outskirts after work until dark, and smiled as wind hit your jacket.

10126.event: A midnight call: your father's heart attack lands him in ICU; you sleep in the corridor, learning to pray.
10126.postEvent: Luckily, he was saved—but your sleep and savings were not.

10127.event: While sorting old things, you found your grandfather's stamps appraised at a jaw-dropping price.

10128.event: Approaching 40, you stopped people-pleasing and quit a chat about face masks in the fridge.

10129.event: The florist said the pothos absorbs bad luck. Its leaves yellowed after three days, then revived when watered, mocking you.
10129.postEvent: You started to believe in the spirit of things—but only for this one pothos that kept going back and forth.

10130.event: On the last subway, you brought home a worn doll; afterward, you always said 'I'm leaving' before going out.
10130.postEvent: Your roommate called it a weird habit, but you felt it understood.

10131.event: You serialized your midlife crisis stories on a forum; they went viral, an editor called as you changed diaper.

10132.event: You shared a last breakfast wrap on divorce day; she added an extra egg for you.
10132.postEvent: She got the house and the kids. You took the old key you had used for seven years.

10133.event: Moving, you found your diary with dried ginkgo leaf and 'I'll make it work anyway.' You laughed to tears.

10134.event: Counting IV bubbles, daughter taped a photo. You died on a sunny day; she felt your hand go empty.

10135.event: This year's physical exam report had two up arrows. The doctor told you to cut back on braised pork.

10136.event: Your pension arrived on time. You figured you'd buy a new backpack for your grandson and felt at ease.

10137.event: Your children and grandchildren visited on the weekend, making the house lively as you cooked many dishes.

10138.event: You learned your colleague John died last night and stared at a yellowed group photo.

10139.event: At dawn tai chi, you saw a fist-sized cold light drift across the lake and vanish, feeling lighter.
10139.postEvent: The next day you went again, but found nothing.

10140.event: Went out to buy groceries in the rain, slipped and fell, broke a bone, and was bedridden for three months.

10141.event: Woke up thirsty, saw a figure like your dead wife, but it was only a coat.

10142.event: Community volunteers taught you to video call, letting you see your faraway granddaughter and making you smile endlessly.

10143.event: You made a will for your modest savings and house, then sighed with relief, feeling settled.

10144.event: You forget things like glasses, then search. Doctor says early Alzheimer's. You stare at the diagnosis.

10145.event: A strange plant glowed blue at night; you made tea from a leaf, slept well, back pain gone.

10146.event: You fought with your son over a nursing home. He left; you smoked all night alone.

10147.event: Sorting books, an incantation slipped out; a blue spark from your finger scorched a page, a past life memory.
10147.postEvent: In the following days, you couldn't stop rubbing your fingers, but nothing happened again.

10148.event: In the dead of night, a heart attack crushes your chest, your arm fails, and the ceiling light blurs.

10149.event: At dusk, a young man passing by at the old street corner resembled your younger self from forty years ago.

10150.event: Every morning you take a slow stroll around the neighborhood and greet old buddies. Your health is still fairly good.

10151.event: Newspaper words grow harder to read; you hold it farther. Age catches everyone.

10152.event: You opened a yellowed photo album; the vivid smiling faces bring bittersweet memories.

10153.event: Family gathers happily at the table during the holidays, and you smile broadly.
10153.postEvent: Your little grandson ran over and kissed you on the cheek. Your heart felt sweeter than honey.

10154.event: A call brings news of Old Li's death, leaving you stunned and hollow.

10155.event: A lawyer came to your home. You calmly distributed your affairs after death. It felt like a heavy burden lifted.
10155.postEvent: The kids seemed relieved too. You felt at peace.

10156.event: You suffered a heart attack. The ambulance arrived with sirens wailing. You brushed with death.
10156.postEvent: Doctor said minutes more would have been too late; you lay on hospital bed with a wry smile.

10157.event: On a peaceful afternoon, you lay in a rocking chair, closed your eyes, and drifted to sleep.

10158.event: You signed up for the community's senior calligraphy class and are learning quite well. You feel young again.

10159.event: You slipped in the bathroom and ended up in the hospital. A broken bone really drains your vitality.
10159.postEvent: After discharge, you need a cane to walk. Life isn't as convenient anymore.

10160.event: You keep dreaming of a misty mountain from your youth, feeling a call, then waking empty.

10161.event: Waking late, you see a faintly glowing plant; it disappears when touched, leaving odd peace.

10162.event: Your children argue fiercely over who should take care of you. You pretend not to hear, but your heart aches.

10163.event: A long-forgotten stock you bought years ago suddenly pays dividends, enough for good tea.

10164.event: After nearly a century, you have clear insight and gladly advise the young.
10164.postEvent: You suddenly feel that the greatest reward of living so long is this understanding.

10165.event: At age {age}, your health index drops to {strength}; the doctor sighs.
10165.postEvent: You start drinking less and download a fitness app.

10166.event: At a reunion, Fatty, once bottom of class, arrives in a Porsche as you worry about mortgage with {money}.
10166.postEvent: You pretend to take a call and leave early.

10167.event: Waiting alone for the last subway, a billboard model winks at you, then goes still.
10167.postEvent: You tell yourself you're just tired, but you quicken your pace each time you pass.

10168.event: You argue with your partner about your child's school until midnight, then storm to the car, charm drops to {charm}.
10168.postEvent: The next day, you both calm down and meet each other halfway.

10169.event: Father hospitalized: you shuttle daily, strained. Health drops below {strength}.
10169.postEvent: You hire a caregiver, but your wallet gets tighter.

10170.event: Laid off, you clutch notice under building as bank texts {money}, sky grays.
10170.postEvent: You start driving for Uber, getting to know the city in the early morning hours again.

10171.event: One morning you pluck your first white hair, staring as {age} flashes through your mind.

10172.event: At an old colleague's funeral, on the way back you decide to finally write that book you always wanted.
10172.postEvent: You buy a new notebook and write the title on the first page.

10173.event: After drinking, you confess your dream of being a rock singer; you both laugh until you cry.
10173.postEvent: The next morning, you find a piece of sheet music you drew beside your pillow, with strange symbols.

10174.event: You find a manuscript, hear ivy breathe, slam it shut in fright, never hear again.
10174.postEvent: You lock the book in a drawer. Occasionally you open it to look, but never read it again.

10175.event: Six months of migraines peak one night; glowing symbols flash, pain vanishes, stamina better than at thirty.
10175.postEvent: The doctor has no explanation, just says you're lucky.

10176.event: Return to the noodle shop where you met on your 20th anniversary; noodles same, her crow's feet bring peace.
10176.postEvent: That night you sleep like a log.

10177.event: You join a woodworking class, discover talent, quiet your mind carving, and earn praise.
10177.postEvent: The night you finish your first chair, you drink up your stash of fine liquor.

10178.event: Strolling at night, you see a green band—news says discharge, but you sense a trapped beast's breath.
10178.postEvent: After that, you start noticing other odd corners of the city.

10179.event: You collapse from overtime, see a glowing flower bloom, then paramedics find no pulse.

10180.event: Born on a stormy night, umbilical cord thrice around your neck, but unharmed.

10181.event: You kept pointing at a corner saying a lady squatted there, so your parents spanked you.

10182.event: In middle school, your tarot and astrology obsession led to secret readings and plummeting grades.

10183.event: You entered a third-tier university, found philosophy boring, dropped out, and started a paranormal blog from your apartment.

10184.event: In an abandoned hospital livestream, your homemade compass spins wildly as a pale green mist figure bows, drawing 100,000 viewers.

10185.event: Your debut book Spirit Notes topped bestseller lists for three weeks, making you a variety show regular.

10186.event: After prolonged exposure to residuals, you coughed all night. Phlegm contained glowing fibers, mana deposits.

10187.event: Late one night, you saw the mirror's secret—your shadow's black mist engulfed you, your last moment.

10188.event: You sold everything, moved to a ruined mountain temple, and daily stared at a mossy stone wall.

10189.event: A faintly glowing spirit deer emerged from a stone wall, met your gaze, and your body dissipated like smoke.

10190.event: Born into a teacher's family in a small Midwest town, a delivery room poster read 'Knowledge changes destiny.'

10191.event: Teacher noticed your math talent, enrolled you in weekly advanced group. You endured two-hour bus ride without complaint.

10192.event: You entered a top provincial middle school, lived in an eight-person dorm, and called your parents weekly.

10193.event: You ranked top 10 in city science exams, then confidently entered a top California CS program.

10194.event: After graduation, you had three tech job offers. On day one, you posted your badge: 'New journey.'

10195.event: In the tech winter, your whole team was laid off; you were coding when HR fired you.

10196.event: Reluctantly, you and two ex-colleagues started a software firm, but a year later it failed, costing you savings and debt.

10197.event: Urged by your parents, you returned home to study, passed the exam, and got a county government job.

10198.event: You married, had a child, promoted to mid-level management. Routine: cafeteria and office. You dreamed of coding.

10199.event: While working late on party documents, you heard ancient chanting from the wall but dismissed it as pipes.

10200.event: Retired, you play chess with your grandson and tell a friend you coded for a few years.

10201.event: One autumn afternoon, you fall asleep in your rocking chair and never wake up.

10202.event: Born into a musical theater family, you were held backstage from one month old, with delicate features.

10203.event: You won gold as Little Red Riding Hood at a drama competition and declared you'd be a star.

10204.event: You enrolled in your dream arts school, stretching and leg pressing daily while skipping academics.

10205.event: Rejected in Juilliard's final round, you got only a community college theater acceptance and cried all night under covers.

10206.event: Starting with $2,000 in New York, you chased your dream from a Brooklyn basement, surviving on one bagel daily.

10207.event: A commercial director liked your interesting face. You filmed small ads and no longer needed family money.

10495.event: The exam switched from English to mana sensitivity, leaving students panicking at the cram-packed temple.

10496.event: Last subway: platform became ancient battlefield; woman with lantern; extra half hour ridden.

10497.event: Emerging economies imported big projects; orders piled up; bonus: model car and 24 months pay.

10498.event: Top celebrities fell to scandals, public taste shifted, gray-uniformed youth filled streets, beauty economy collapsed.

10499.event: Moon base found ancient aether engine; science booms; you take night courses in runic engineering.

10500.event: After a random attack at intercity station, you stare at strangers' bags at security, losing two hours of sleep nightly.

10501.event: Gene therapy covered by insurance; degenerative diseases cured. Alzheimer's patient reversed symptoms.

10502.event: Unknown creatures gnawed global undersea cables, causing a 72-hour blackout; you found cards and learned games with family by candlelight.

10503.event: The oil cartel collapsed, prices fell below cost, half your oil-town shops closed, and old neighbors moved to the coast.

10504.event: A revival of Han clothing led your company to require weekend Confucian rehearsals, but your robe selfie got many likes.

10505.event: After heavy rain, park grass glowed blue; pollen caused three-day fish dreams and long clinic lines.

10506.event: The government pilots a basic income covering rent; you quit your toxic boss for a sculpture class.

10507.event: During a heatwave blackout, you saw a strange star and died peacefully smiling.

10508.event: Black market half-copy of Qi art; coworker practiced, got itchy head bumps; hospital clueless.

10509.event: Saw two shadows under streetlight; one faint vanished; then heard whispers in wind.

10510.event: Using an ancient prescription, you condensed qi, but missed the stray cat's shifting gaze.

10511.event: While walking, a sinister force invaded your mind, devoured your memories, and killed you.

10512.event: Jade pendant rebounded dark cultivator's soul; you gained memories and power, felt worldview off.

10513.event: You forced a heavenly tribulation, but a purple thunderbolt turned you to charcoal and scattered your soul.

10514.event: After nine lightning strikes tempered your body, you shone and ascended, later reported as a suspected UFO kite.

10515.event: In deep meditation, you felt millennia pass in an instant, gaining unearthly insights.

10516.event: Obsessed with ancient qi-gathering, you sense residual embers, force qi, suffer backlash, your consciousness dark, glimpsing a spark flee, losing yourself.

10517.event: Studied three months, passed civil service exam; first day at work, felt life set.

10518.event: It started with a carton of cigarettes, then grew until inspectors came and the gavel fell.

10519.event: Your poverty project became a national TV model, earning promotions; even your teacher called you boss.

10520.event: First 5k run threw up; sergeant yelled keep running; that started your transformation.

10521.event: During that mine clearance mission, you defused sixteen landmines. On the seventeenth, everything went black. You never woke up.

10522.event: A bullet pierced your vest in a border conflict; you fell, heard a muffled call, and never woke.

10523.event: You won the All-Army Competition, became the legendary King of Soldiers, and at fifty still trained rookies with vigor.

10524.event: Your PhD algorithm sold to a tech giant for millions. On signing, you still tuned; advisor said you're the boss.

10525.event: After a biology PhD, you taught high school; a student asked if clones would destroy the world.

10526.event: Routinely cultured cells, ran gels, wrote papers; finally associate professor. At dinner, everyone toasted promotion; you smiled silently.

10527.event: Your Science paper solved a decades-old problem; your alma mater built a statue, but you'd prefer funding.

10528.event: Stray cats tripled, walked upright; authorities blamed disease; you knew mana increased tenfold.

10529.event: The day the market circuit breaker halved your account brought layoffs and colleagues in hallways calling spouses about layoffs.

10530.event: The plague killed many including you; after a night outside the ER, your neighbor died, you died, hand sanitizer unopened.

10531.event: Nuclear fusion commercial; oil crashed; gas stations to charging. You thought no gas tax; self-driving car splashed you.

10532.event: Giant humanoid figures fought in clouds; incense lit spontaneously; you made a wish on your balcony.

10533.event: At {age}, drew crooked summoning circle with red chalk; ants marched through it next day.
10533.postEvent: The science teacher explained that the calcium carbonate in the chalk attracted the ants, but you didn't believe it.

10534.event: Turning TV to static at night and whispering your name yields coins the next day.
10534.postEvent: Those coins were enough to buy a popsicle. But later your mom said the TV was broken.

10535.event: Prettiest girl wore your color all week. You thought it was magic, but her mom made clothes from two colors.

10536.event: One silkworm refused mulberry leaves, spun silk under full moon, wove faint blue cocoon.
10536.postEvent: The cocoon later disappeared. Your mom said a rat took it.

10537.event: A masked boy your age moved next door, hiding in impossible places like the fridge without feeling cold.
10537.postEvent: You later learned he had a congenital disease causing low temperature, yet he still felt like porcelain.

10538.event: You tried beef-flavored dog food and liked it, but your dog barked at your rice all night.

10539.event: You find a SIM-less old phone; nightly at 7 it predicts trivial events: "umbrella for puddles", "old shoes for run".
10539.postEvent: It was incredibly accurate. But you lost it and never found it again.

10540.event: In a blinking contest, your reflection blinked when you didn't, scaring you from mirrors.

10541.event: You made a Play-Doh person, left it, it vanished, then found intact in a park sandbox with tiny footprints.

10542.event: Poured perfume into fish tank; fish died; dad revived them; one survived, ate only from your hand.

10543.event: You saw your double under the bed with hair parted opposite; family dismissed it, but your cousin's look said otherwise.
10543.postEvent: Later, your mom had someone come and chant some prayers, and you never saw it again.

10544.event: Your slow-growing sunflower turned to face an empty window on the neighbor's roof.

10545.event: Broke grandpa's watch, read 'Don't turn around' and screamed. Then saw 'Just kidding, love Grandpa.'

10546.event: A fish seller gave you a free koi you talked to daily; it lived so long you felt understood.
10546.postEvent: The day it died, you graduated from elementary school.

10547.event: During kindergarten naptime you dreamed of becoming a butterfly, woke on the ceiling, and fell when the teacher screamed.
10547.postEvent: The teacher told everyone you were a sleepwalker, but you remembered the lightness—it wasn't a dream.

10548.event: You picked a humming flower that withered at dusk, and cried your heart out.

10549.event: Your first spicy snack made you cry, yet you reached for another, addicted to the thrill.

10550.event: You bet a neighbor kid you'd eat strawberry toothpaste, then needed an ER stomach pump.
10550.postEvent: Your parents never bought fruity toothpaste again, and to this day the smell of strawberries makes you gag.

10551.event: A lady in white led you through the mall to your parents, but your mom said you were always alone.

10552.event: You drew a crayon door on the wall, found it ajar with gray light at night, and hid under covers till dawn.

10553.event: At the kindergarten talent show, you stood still as a tree, impressing the teacher, but forgot your moves.

10554.event: Every night you check under the bed for monsters, until you touch something wet and soft and never look again.

10555.event: While bathing, you saw a child's face in water; mom said it was a bubble, but it was your own.

10556.event: You dismantled the remote to build a mana detector, but got a spanking and a bill for a new remote.
10556.postEvent: Your dad said your inventions always ended up expensive.

10557.event: Scared by your first horror movie into sleeping with lights on, you later became a paranormal enthusiast.

10558.event: You found an ice-cold ancient coin at the alley, dreamed of clouds, then it vanished.

10559.event: Your family pretended your invisible friend Ling existed, and on moving day, his door slammed shut as you waved goodbye.

10560.event: While mom was out, you painted lips red and eyelids green; a neighbor called you a star, you preened.

10561.event: While studying late, you heard a broken harmonica, saw a figure vanish, then memorized faster.

10562.event: You and your roommate played apple-peeling; the peel broke. You laughed and slept. Next day, toothbrush on toilet lid.

10563.event: Customer asked for a dream drink; you made grape tea, and she tipped double.

10564.event: In subway, you chased a figure like an old classmate; they chatted, smiled, told you to take care too.

10565.event: A used bookstore find: a 1999 diary whose author's daily life eerily mirrors yours, even the dumpling filling for lunch.

10566.event: During campus lockdown, you grew balcony garlic, sang to it, and heard a faint hum at harvest.

10567.event: You stumbled into a live stream, only viewer. Host recited sutra all night; you fell asleep and woke refreshed.

10568.event: You drew a card: 'The last human hears a knock.' Then you heard three knocks; camera showed nothing.

10569.event: A vending machine gave you an extra drink labeled "For you. Don't tell anyone." It was the most thirst-quenching ever.

10570.event: An old man claimed your shoelace was loose, but it was tied. He vanished; a missing poster resembled him.

10571.event: A peeled boiled egg appeared daily at your spot. You ate it; a note asked if it was good.

10572.event: You photographed the moon, saw a spacesuit figure, then it vanished. Now the night sky seems to wave.

10573.event: All clocks were seven minutes slow; you were late unpunished, then time normalized unnoticed.

10574.event: You held a Free Hugs sign. One girl hugged you and gave a fruit candy. Wrapper sparkled at sunset.

10575.event: You bought hot pot snacks; an egg in shell floated in broth. You and cashier exchanged glances, pretended nothing.
10575.postEvent: Later, when you went back to that store, the broth of the hot pot snacks always looked a little different.

10576.event: Returning late, elevator stops at an unselected floor. Empty hall, old shoes aligned pointing toward elevator.
10576.postEvent: You never saw those shoes again, but the elevator still sometimes stops at that floor for no reason.

10577.event: You paid real money for metaverse land; a year later, it was seized and became a data graveyard.
10577.postEvent: You log into a virtual world; your character is stuck on a wasteland edge with a 'Pending Demolition' tag.

10578.event: One slice in a supermarket bread pack bears a faint '合格' like a stamp but is ink-printed.
10578.postEvent: You ate that slice. It was a bit chewy, but nothing special.

10579.event: A sidewalk fortune teller grabbed your arm, claiming a past life debt, so you scanned his QR code.
10579.postEvent: The fortune teller took your money and gave you a talisman reading Debt cleared. Next time don't forget your wallet.

10580.event: The office pantry microwave self-activates at 3 a.m. due to lab mana fluctuations interfering with circuits.
10580.postEvent: Eventually you learn to put milk in at night, finding it warm by morning.

10581.event: You buy premium noise-canceling headphones, yet faintly hear plants growing in a neighbor's fish tank.
10581.postEvent: You post online and get called crazy, but an audio engineer says it might be bioelectrical signals.

10582.event: You never spoke in WeChat group '26 Still Believing in Light' sharing Ultraman memes daily; it disbanded after 2 years.
10582.postEvent: After the group disbanded, you found 'People Over 27 Still Believing in Light' but didn't join.

10583.event: A rooster that crows precisely vanishes; its owner blames residual mana. You find a chicken footprint in your porridge bowl.
10583.postEvent: After that, occasional grains of unhulled rice appear in your porridge. The owner says they're gifts left by the rooster.

10584.event: You sold your old phone, and the buyer's blurred ID photo address was your old home from ten years ago.
10584.postEvent: Though you never reply, that address recalls faint childhood footsteps in that old house.

10585.event: Dream of overtime in strange office for a week. Wake with sore wrists, see same mug in abandoned building.
10585.postEvent: You muster courage, go inside, and find a note: 'Today is another day of leaving on time.'

10586.event: You find a diary on a park bench recording a future life in your handwriting.
10586.postEvent: You turn to the last page, which reads: Today I found an old diary on a park bench.

10587.event: You develop a screen allergy causing nosebleeds; a herbalist says your spiritual vision awakened but your body can't cope.
10587.postEvent: The herbalist's remedy stops nosebleeds, but screen letters occasionally wriggle like worms.

10588.event: Niche app match 'Deleted Account' sends weather, claims to be the suit in your closet with tags.
10588.postEvent: You open your closet; the suit still has tags, but a wet-ink forecast sits in its pocket.

10589.event: Born into a warm blue glow, you see blurry specks like fireflies, then a nurse lifts you, and they vanish.

10590.event: Crawling from living room to kitchen, you hit fridge corner. Forehead feels soft like Jell-O. Mom finds you giggling.

10591.event: Crying nonstop late at night, mom hums an unfamiliar tune. You quiet, stare at curtains, sensing someone humming along.

10592.event: On your first carousel ride, at the peak you feel weightless, though others say it ran normally.

10593.event: During a walk, you point at an empty alley, shout for your dead grandpa, then stare silently.

10594.event: Playing with a wind-up frog, it jumps, stops, turns, then hops back. You clap; mom dozes.

10595.event: The nurse gives your first shot. You wail, but when the needle pierces, a rainbow halo flashes and pain fades.

10596.event: In the bath, bubbles form a ball in your hand. Mom lifts you, and it pops with a boop.

10597.event: On your second birthday, you blow out candles. One rekindles a blue flame then dies. Dad mutters they're defective.

10598.event: At daycare, you talk to ants by an anthill. Caregiver hears you ask their origin; ants line and circle you.

10599.event: At the farmers' market, you see ripples like a transparent figure twisting inside the fish tank.

10600.event: Found a maple leaf, listened, said it tells an old house's story, then put it in a book. Gone.

10601.event: Woke with fever, mother wiped you. Saw walls transparent, revealing murmuring threads. Fever broke; seemed a dream.

10602.event: After learning to walk, you lunged, hovered caught, grandmother gasped. You grinned; drool made a heart.

10603.event: A woman like your late grandmother gave you a note to water plants you don’t have.
10603.postEvent: After that, every time you passed a flower shop, you inexplicably bought a potted plant to take home.

10604.event: At team-building, you enter fog, an old phone booth rings, and your childhood self warns: don't buy that doomed house.
10604.postEvent: You hesitated and gave up; six months later, that development was abandoned.

10605.event: After drinking a smiley face latte, you dream of being a cat chasing a glowing butterfly on rooftops.

10606.event: On your birthday you got an anonymous VHS tape showing ten years of weather forecasts, ending with purple snow.
10606.postEvent: You locked the tape in a drawer. Occasionally you took it out to check, and every forecast came true.

10607.event: A rabbit-suited subway worker hands you a balloon showing your shrinking bank balance.
10607.postEvent: You hurriedly checked your account; everything was normal. But the balloon's number burst when it hit zero.

10608.event: Woke on park bench, found rusty key, unlocked room; mirror showed you in dynasty robe.
10608.postEvent: You stared for a few seconds. When you pushed the door again, there was only a blank wall.

10609.event: At class reunion, former valedictorian whispered, 'Can you see me?' You noticed his translucent body; others clinked glasses.
10609.postEvent: He smiled and vanished as you blinked; the number he left became a dead line.

10610.event: Walking at night, dog barked at blank wall. Water beads seeped, forming 'Leave'. By morning dried; wall icy cold.
10610.postEvent: You later kept an eye on the news. Nothing good ever came from that direction again.

10611.event: At the gym, barbell plates buzzed, your vision blurred, then you saw flames—but you had two on your shoulders.
10611.postEvent: This vision only lasted a few seconds, but from then on you could vaguely sense the emotional temperature of others.

10612.event: Found a diary: 'You'll meet someone in a red raincoat.' Then rain and a crimson figure appeared.
10612.postEvent: You never dared approach them, but the figure appeared nightly at different corners until you moved.

10613.event: The teller's touch showed her gas death; you urged her to order takeout.
10613.postEvent: The next day's paper didn't run the story, but that teller always expedited your requests afterward.

10614.event: Extra elevator button B4 led to blue hallway and 'Fate Archives' door; heard pages rustling.
10614.postEvent: Later you quietly asked the building management, and they said B4 was the storage level, sealed off long ago.

10615.event: Hollow pumpkin contained a note about being a tree. Laughed; later dreamed of a banyan tree with deep roots.

10616.event: Sleepless, you see a forum post 'Whose cat texts?
10616.postEvent: After opening a can that night, you often got random character strings from the cat on your phone early.

10617.event: One weekend morning, you found unclaimed soy milk; it tasted normal and never returned.

10618.event: Finding a twenty-year-old ticket with an unfamiliar note, you cannot recall that day.

10619.event: During a team trip, a girl in pajamas smiled and walked into wall. Owner said no children were there.
10619.postEvent: After that, whenever you worked late, you subconsciously avoided that hallway.

10620.event: You obsessed over supermarket stickers, spent a month for a plastic lunchbox, then lost interest and never used it.

10621.event: For a week, ginkgo tree hole played music box melody. Stopped when approached; then sigh and silence.

10622.event: An old fortune teller gave you a rusty coin, said "One more," then vanished.
10622.postEvent: You kept the coin in a drawer, occasionally taking it out to examine, but never figured out its meaning.

10623.event: After a vitamin D deficiency diagnosis, you sunbathe daily and notice a woman across waving, though you've never spoken.

10624.event: Meditation: imagine as stone. Dreamed of century as boulder. Woke with sore back, feeling close to stones.
10624.postEvent: Now every time you pass a stone block, you give it a second glance.

10625.event: In underground parking, elevator went B2 to 3rd with applause; doors opened to no one, only broken glasses.
10625.postEvent: You never took that elevator again.

10626.event: Your partner made herbal-embroidered masks; an old lady said they block bad things. Now stored, you have occasional nightmares.

10627.event: You bought a blank book for $5; moonlight shows "Stay longest on page 47," but it has 46 pages.
10627.postEvent: Now you lock the book in a drawer every night, but you always feel it turning its own pages.

10628.event: On 41st birthday, you learned unicycle, fell for 3 months, wobbled 5 meters, got 100 likes, then stored it.

10629.event: The water cooler's gurgling spelled 'LET ME OUT' in Morse code, but no one would replace it.
10629.postEvent: After that, you always went to another water cooler.

10630.event: A stranger praised your terrible chess, left a note to meet at night. You stayed up instead.

10631.event: An NPC with your name, wearing yesterday's clothes, stood outside your building; then a night screenshot of your bedroom arrived.
10631.postEvent: From then on, you never opened that game after midnight.

10632.event: You subbed for a sick classmate in a debate, quoted unknown philosophy, then remembered nothing though your voice was normal.
10632.postEvent: Later you found out the contact lenses you wore that day were actually your late mother's keepsake.

10633.event: During a summer job at a video store, you find an unlabeled tape of your father as a boy.
10633.postEvent: At the end of the tape, the boy said, 'Son, don't tell your mom you're working here.'

10634.event: Flag raiser: snapped rope dropped flag on you; you saw extra star, others said unchanged.
10634.postEvent: From then on, every time you see the flag, you briefly can't tell red from green.

10635.event: Your pet quail escaped; months later it nested on your perfect physics test, though you never passed.
10635.postEvent: You studied the test paper carefully and found clear solution steps for every problem.

10636.event: After you shared your TV's midnight auto-on at the Ghost Club, it broke the next day and never did it again.
10636.postEvent: Club members agreed it was your first 'exorcism' experience.

10637.event: Math exam: you drew hexagram, wrote 'Gods help', got full marks, note: 'Nice drawing, answer better'.
10637.postEvent: You have no memory of writing any solution steps.

10638.event: Studying late, your hated étude from next door; you knocked, music stopped. Neighbor later said no piano, family out.
10638.postEvent: You pressed your ear to the wall; behind it was only a closet.

10639.event: In a used test prep book, you found a note; drawing an X turned its problems into next week's exam.
10639.postEvent: You ranked first in your grade, but the book's pages turned at night, reading 'You owe me one.'

10640.event: You secretly used your mom's makeup for a selfie; a stranger replied with her identical young photo.
10640.postEvent: Mom said that bottle of foundation was leftover from when she was eighteen, and the account's avatar was static noise.

10641.event: During silent flash mobs, you freeze and see your future dorm on a glass wall, then normalcy returns.
10641.postEvent: You got into that university; the dorm matched your vision, except for an unclaimed pothos on the windowsill.

10642.event: You casually told your dying plant that if it lived, you'd rank top three, and it sprouted into an unknown "Unspeakable Wood" within a month.
10642.postEvent: You got third in exams, but each night faint whispers from the balcony recite your formulas.

10643.event: Found a worn plush rabbit; it altered your alarm. Returned it, but it came back wearing your sock.
10643.postEvent: You buried it behind the school; next day a peach tree grew from the grave, blooming one flower in spring.

10644.event: You found a strange cassette, converted to MP3, heard a hum, and saw silver thread veins in the room.
10644.postEvent: Grandpa recorded aether echoes as a youth and was surprised you could hear them.

10645.event: Your med bag had exact time and 'Take facing clock tower.' Pharmacist said printing error.
10645.postEvent: After that, every time you took the medicine, you still instinctively glanced toward the clock tower.

10646.event: Each day for a week, the billboard model seemed to shift slightly toward you at the red light.
10646.postEvent: You took a detour at the next intersection, adding five minutes but easing your mind.

10647.event: You scan old photos, but one always gains a blurry figure in the window behind you, though the original has none.
10647.postEvent: You saved that photo alone in an encrypted folder and didn't tell your family.

10648.event: After the reunion, you found an unclaimed house key shaped like a lock from twenty years ago.

10649.event: You saw your slippers neatly by the toilet, though they were under the bed before sleep.
10649.postEvent: From then on, you subconsciously lined up your shoes neatly every night before bed before you could feel at ease.

10650.event: At the laundromat, you saw white feathers spinning with your laundry, but they vanished when you opened the door.

10651.event: You craved red bean ice from a café your spouse said was demolished, but you saw it last week.

10652.event: A balcony cherry tomato grew twin fused fruits like butts; friends said your Photoshop skills declined.

10653.event: An unknown number texts 'Stop digging.' You've only planted onions and dug a compost hole.
10653.postEvent: After hesitating, you filled the hole back in and planted the green onions in a pot instead.

10654.event: Every bus card tap caused a second-longer beep; service center said the data was normal.

10655.event: Doing tai chi, a young man asks to be your apprentice, citing a "vortex", but you dismiss it as arthritis.

10656.event: You found a 90s pop cassette; between songs, faint whispers that vanished when rewound.
10656.postEvent: You put that tape back in the deepest corner of the drawer and never took it out again.

10657.event: At the supermarket, the scale undercharged you a dime; you left uneasy but with a bargain.

10658.event: You dreamed of stairs; mirrors showed you at different ages. Top mirror touched glass; you woke with cold palm.

10659.event: While stargazing after retirement, you saw a meteor shower form into your ID number.
10659.postEvent: Your spouse rolled her eyes, but you dreamed stars came to your door for tea.

10660.event: The dishes you cook form strange patterns, like shredded pork always an odd number, unchangeable.
10660.postEvent: You serve the dish; your family ignores it, but your granddaughter says it's staring at her.

10661.event: Your bone density is 30% higher; you recall handling glowing green powder as a child.
10661.postEvent: Most of those coworkers are gone now, and your bones occasionally give off a faint glow at night.

10662.event: Every dusk a young man on the bench, resembling your young father, reads and adjusts his glasses.
10662.postEvent: You want to approach, but he always folds his paper on time and disappears into the fog ahead.

10663.event: While livestreaming, you accidentally activate a virtual streamer that voices your long-held secrets.
10663.postEvent: You're startled and quickly turn it off, but later you can't help opening that app whenever you want to vent.

10664.postEvent: You search the entire stall but find no missing pages. For days after, you're afraid to open your bedroom door.

10665.event: A silent, sunglasses-wearing new player plays badly, leaving a strange gray-white card each time.
10665.postEvent: You secretly pocket one; at night the card flips in the drawer as your name emerges.

10666.event: Learned from granddaughter, you quickly master "juejuezi" and "yyds", but later realize your memes predict weather: "dayuanzhong" foretells a downpour.
10666.postEvent: Your wife signs you up as a weather observer volunteer. You're caught between laughter and tears.

10667.event: You answered a question; your reply was deleted as 'too perfect' and flagged AI. You don't recall writing it.
10667.postEvent: You search your browser history but find nothing. A chill runs down your spine.

10668.event: Your late spouse's sewing machine runs alone at midnight, unplugged, with your name embroidered on cloth under the needle.
10668.postEvent: Placing the cloth in a drawer stops the noise, but a thin light leaks from its crack nightly.

10669.event: Falling behind on a senior choir outing, you hear factory radio calisthenics from a motionless sparrow.
10669.postEvent: You finish the routine, the sparrow flies away, leaving a coin stamped with your birth date.

10670.event: You found a blue pill engraved 'FORGET' and flushed it. Next morning, three more appeared on your desk.
10670.postEvent: You buried the pills in a flowerpot. The flowers that grew were white and memoryless.

10671.event: While doing tai chi in the park, friends' moves are mirror opposites. They insist they've always done it this way.
10671.postEvent: You stand frozen, looking down to see your own shadow has assumed the opposite starting pose.

10672.event: In a hospital corridor, you see your own face; it vanishes through a wall. That night, your heart stops peacefully.
10672.postEvent: When the nurse finds you, a gardenia flower that never grows in the hospital lies on your pillow.

10673.event: Finding a burial gown embroidered with your name and today's date, you put it on and die that night.
10673.postEvent: The coroner rules natural aging, but a strange smile lingers on your lips.

10674.event: You find your missing diary page in a newspaper, with unfamiliar handwriting asking how you are.
10674.postEvent: You stick it on the fridge. Every time you walk past, you feel it watching you.

10675.event: Annual checkup: aether residue 3000x normal. Nurse says glitch, but you see her eyeball rotate fully.
10675.postEvent: You secretly retest three times; the value keeps rising. On the last report, nothing is written except a smiley face.

10676.event: Feeding pigeons in a park, a lame one scratches 'HELP' on the ground, then all stare at you.
10676.postEvent: You flee in panic, return with food, but find no pigeons—only droppings forming the same words.

10677.event: Grandson gives smartphone. You ask voice assistant 'How much longer?' It replies in late wife's voice: 'I'm waiting.'
10677.postEvent: That night you bury the phone. Next morning, from your pillow: 'Good morning, it will rain, bring umbrella.'

10678.event: On a Senior Tour, your reflection turns away while all others smile at the camera.
10678.postEvent: You're not in the photo, but seniors insist you stood at the edge.

10679.event: Handwritten letter: woman in red dress from abandoned building is still waiting.
10679.postEvent: You don't remember any abandoned building, but in midnight dreams, you always smell damp cement.

10680.event: Old leather shoes you wanted 30 years ago appear at doorstep. They fit. Note: 'You've walked far, worked hard.'
10680.postEvent: You lost the sticky note, but those shoes always led you back to your old factory.

10681.event: The noodle shop owner gave you plain noodles, saying it was from your mom's stall that vanished 50 years ago.
10681.postEvent: You finish the soup and see your name's initials in scallions at the bowl's bottom.

10682.event: At 3 AM, woken by the TV playing your wedding video, with all guests applauding except your younger self warning.
10682.postEvent: You feigned illness to skip the gathering; news reported a fatal gas explosion at the venue.

10683.event: While watering flowers on the balcony, the lightning rod bent toward you, then stilled; your orchid had a scorched hole.
10683.postEvent: From then on, every time it thundered, the orchid would tremble slightly, as if trying to speak.

10686.postEvent: You don't recall who this is, yet whenever a chill runs down your spine, you instinctively say "Stop it".

10687.event: A sycamore leaf silently appeared each morning until you tucked it into an album.

10688.event: The new caregiver hums a familiar tune; one sleepless night you recall it from your mother, a secret you kept.

10690.event: At night you got water and saw your reflection wink and shush you.

10692.event: At your 90th birthday, kids on phones, so you took the biggest cake slice and ate it alone.

10693.event: You dozed off; a schoolboy asked about homework, then vanished as you jolted awake.

10694.event: Your great-granddaughter used your dentures as a Barbie crown and dragged you to her coronation.

10695.event: On a foggy morning, an old man flew a kite pulling a purple cloud, then vanished in ten minutes.

10696.event: Marry me in the next life.
