11029.event: At the bank, a man smiled. The teller said you got $888.88 subsidy as a witness of Aether Decay.

11030.event: You dreamed of an abandoned station, but your spouse stopped you. You woke with a ticket to "Mortal Era".

11031.event: At {age}, you ordered a cake with 'Just alive is fine.' It had an EKG line. Everyone laughed.
11031.postEvent: The nearby customers laughed, and you laughed too.

11032.event: Working from home, upstairs drilling paused for half an hour of chanting; neighbor is a programmer, not Buddhist.

11033.event: Your front camera showed a transparent figure behind you. It vanished when you turned around.
11033.postEvent: You open the camera again, the figure gone, only your overly smoothed face remains.

11034.event: At a school reunion, you contest everyone's class trip memory, then awkwardly find yours includes red sneakers you never owned.
11034.postEvent: You fell silent for a moment, then decided to blame it on {age}-year-old memory decline.

11035.event: After mutual fund losses, your coin rolled into a temple drain. A monk said cut losses.
11035.postEvent: You silently put away the finance app on your phone.

11036.event: You found an orange cat with a USB drive. The file had repeated Chinese characters for 'sleepy'.
11036.postEvent: You hang the USB drive on the cat's neck; it licks a paw and walks off without looking back.

11037.event: During insomnia, you saw three identical live streams of an empty room. The screenshot vanished.
11037.postEvent: You tried to recall the layout of that living room, but the details blurred into a mess in your mind.

11038.event: Haunted by a pocket watch countdown, at zero your breath and heartbeat stopped, the world went silent.

11039.event: Rear-ended by a robed old man, he gave you a card reading "Underworld Chauffeur" that then vanished.
11039.postEvent: The repair shop said the impact couldn't damage the chassis, but you still felt a chill.

11040.event: A fortune teller refunded your money, saying your fate is mass-produced.
11040.postEvent: You held the returned $50, unsure whether to be happy or disappointed.

11041.event: A call from Destiny Archives Follow-up asked for a rating; pressing 3 cost {random} yuan and ended the call.
11041.postEvent: You checked the bill and found the charge listed as 'Existence Tax.'

11042.event: You saw elderly doing Tai Chi with faint fireflies at their fingertips, but your companion saw nothing.
11042.postEvent: You blinked, and the sparkles were gone, leaving only the old folks squinting in the blazing sun.

11043.event: At {age}, you saw your shadow lag, so you walked in sunlight more.
11043.postEvent: The shadow still misses a beat occasionally, like a delayed surveillance feed.

11044.event: You coughed up your late wife's favorite blue flower, smiled, and closed your eyes.
11044.postEvent: The coroner ruled natural organ failure, and the blue flower in your palm turned to ash.

11045.event: At {age}, you find a candy under your pillow daily. You sleepwalk to place it; the brand is gone.
11045.postEvent: Later you tried one. It was so sweet it didn't taste like anything from this world.

11046.event: In a nursing home garden, you found a note you never wrote, but handwriting proves it's yours.

11047.event: For a week, you woke at 3:33 AM. A sign pointed to 'Origin.' You followed it to a breakfast shop.
11047.postEvent: The soy milk there tasted exactly like it did fifty years ago.

11048.event: You found an unsent letter in an old book, from you to you, postmarked tomorrow.
11048.postEvent: You waited all day, but the mailman never came. However, the letter appeared by itself on your pillow.

11049.event: You've smelled a beloved floral scent for seven days, but doctors insist it's only a hallucination.

11050.event: Your daily walks downstairs traced a curled cat shape, discovered by your grandson via a map app.

11051.event: You once understood stray cats predicting deaths; after Grandma Zhang died, they left, and you lost the ability.
11051.postEvent: Grandma Zhang passed peacefully. Her family said she looked like she was just asleep.

11052.event: Shaking her hand, you saw her life story. You blurted it out; now she only touches you with gloves.

11053.event: Your granddaughter gave you an MP3 player. The only song made you cry; no one else heard it.
11053.postEvent: Later you tried humming it to the doctor. The doctor suggested you listen less to nostalgic golden oldies.

11054.event: For your 90th birthday, relatives seemed like mannequins; caregiver said no one came.
11054.postEvent: A new family photo appeared on the nightstand, and you didn't recognize anyone in it.

11055.event: A gray pigeon thanked you for grain from eighty years ago, recalling the pigeon you saved at six.

11056.event: You unconsciously draw complex mandalas on newspapers. They look like temple mandalas.

11057.event: Every night that winter, a motherly voice called your nickname. You answered once and never woke.

11058.event: At your 100th party, candles turned blue, reflecting your young face, then your great-grandson blew them out.
11058.postEvent: Inside the cut cake was a fruit candy with a wrapper from a brand discontinued forty years ago.

11059.event: You sat in a cart as a man popped bubble wrap at the entrance, hypnotizing you while others ignored it.
11059.postEvent: You later discovered that man came to buy bubble wrap every Thursday, rain or shine.

11060.event: You found a lottery ticket in a puddle, won five dollars, but it expired yesterday.
11060.postEvent: The clerk smiled and said, "Not even a minute late."

11061.event: Woken by a kitchen rustle, your mom carried you to see a mouse pushing half a cookie toward you.
11061.postEvent: The next day, the cookie was gone.

11062.event: At a used bookstore, you found a book where each page read 'Nice weather, a good day to forget.'
11062.postEvent: When you put the book down and went outside, the sky was overcast.

11063.event: An old man said you were a tree for 300 years, then left.
11063.postEvent: Later, when you went back, there was never any stall in that spot again.

11064.event: With your mom on the subway, an old lady's knitting unraveled as she left; everyone ignored the blue yarn.

11065.event: A plastic crystal bought by Mom revived a nearly dead pothos.

11066.event: You fed a stray cat with your dad. A rusty coin appeared and the cat vanished.
11066.postEvent: Your dad showed it to someone who said it was a coin from the Victorian era, not valuable but interesting.

11067.event: At a convenience store, the clerk gave you a hot meal, saying someone ordered it. The store was empty.

11068.event: You find a notebook on a bench; its pages sketch your back reading it.
11068.postEvent: You whipped your head around, but only saw swaying tree shadows behind you.

11069.event: You passed a missing person poster; the photo was a younger you, with your childhood nickname.
11069.postEvent: You tore down the poster, and the back was blank.

11070.event: Washing hands, your reflection moved a beat slower, smiled, then returned to normal.

11071.event: You ignored a warning not to wear blue, then suffered coffee spills, bird droppings, and a wind gust.
11071.postEvent: Since then, you've had more respect for strange text messages.

11072.event: A farmer's market vendor charged you $7.30, then added a tomato saying it dreamed of you.
11072.postEvent: When you got home and cut the tomato, the seed arrangement inside looked a bit like a smiling face.

11073.event: At the 18th floor, a figure entered from a dark hallway, pressed 1, vanished in sunlight at the first floor.

11074.event: A photo showed you with a forgotten toy. Mom confirmed you had it. It later appeared under your bed.
11074.postEvent: There wasn't a speck of dust on it.

11075.event: You heard footsteps outside your 12th-floor window at 3 AM, but saw only a cigarette butt on the AC.

11076.event: On the bus, you scoffed at a koi luck post, then broke a heel and stepped in gum.

11077.event: At the bank, the ticket reads a line besides the queue number: 214th cycle, good work.
11077.postEvent: The teller glanced and said "System failure, happens often," and you noticed his badge number was 214.

11078.event: A balloon seller had a balloon with your face. He said you lost it at age seven.
11078.postEvent: You bought it, let it go, and it flew straight toward your childhood home.

11079.event: After losing ten coins, the claw machine moved on its own, grabbed a plushie, and declared you the 10,000th loser.

11080.event: Night laundry: you find a strange woman's blouse in the washer. You didn't buy it. Tag says tomorrow's date.
11080.postEvent: The next day, you saw a woman on the street wearing the exact same blouse from your washer.

11081.event: A gust of wind swept a sheet from the roof to a balcony rack, landing perfectly.

11082.event: A package from twenty years ago holds a tape of you singing and asking how you are.
11082.postEvent: You left no reply on the tape.

11083.event: Flickering lights spelled 'I'm here' in Morse code until a bulb change fixed it.
11083.postEvent: The new bulb's packaging read: 'Shelf life: until next spirit tide.'

11084.event: On a full moon, you unconsciously grinned your first smile, and the whole family gathered around the crib, delighted.
11084.postEvent: That photo was revisited by your parents many times.

11085.event: You suddenly cried all night, inconsolable. The old folks muttered you might have seen something unclean.
11085.postEvent: At dawn, you fell into deep sleep as if nothing had happened.

11086.event: At eight months, a 104°F fever nearly became pneumonia, terrifying your family.

11087.event: The first time you clearly said 'Mom,' your mother dropped a hanger, knelt, and hugged you, crying.

11088.event: One afternoon alone in your crib, you drew circles as the ceiling lamp suddenly swayed.
11088.postEvent: No one noticed, and you giggled.

11089.event: The loud renovation drill next door scared you, causing sleepless nights and weight loss.

11090.event: You worked late; streetlights died behind you. Your shadow stood still, waving slowly.
11090.postEvent: The next day, your colleagues found you under a streetlight—no breath.

11091.event: Doctor says stage IV pancreatic cancer; you think of mortgage payments left.
11091.postEvent: Three months later, you passed away quietly in the hospital, your daughter holding your hand as the monitor flatlined.

11092.event: One day mirror-gazing, you catch your shadow making a 'shush' gesture behind you, but your hands hang still.
11092.postEvent: You closed your eyes forever before you could react.

11093.event: A press machine crushed your right hand on night shift; infection proved fatal.

11094.event: A wealthy businessman hired you to find his missing daughter; mana led to her remains, and karmic debt killed you.

11095.event: In your final livestream, you claimed the spiritual embers were dying, deleted your account, and vanished.

11096.event: A famous psychic streamer suffered nightly nightmares, then collapsed and died at the desk.

11097.event: During a livestream, you lost your voice, saw faces, had a breakdown, and died in the hospital a week later.

11098.event: You became a dark web legend selling visionsight potions, but addiction led to a fatal overdose.

11099.event: The drug's side effects caused intermittent blindness as mana threads wrapped around his body, suffocating him.

11100.event: His debugging tool went rogue, trapping his soul forever in the code it generated.

11101.event: After fixing the bronze mirror, you vanished into its world; sometimes people saw extra bronze-green eyes.

11102.event: After fainting while fixing an old clock, I woke, locked myself in for three days, and found restored items dull.

11103.event: You died after a minor celestial tribulation severed your meridians and you coughed blood for months.

11104.event: While patrolling the border, you were ambushed and took fatal shrapnel to the spine protecting comrades.

11106.event: You were severely wounded in an operation and died despite rescue efforts.

11107.event: While practicing sorcery in an abandoned factory, you encountered another practitioner.

11108.event: Sensing celestial tribulation, you send your mother money and ascend peak alone, glimpsing golden light through cloud rift.

11109.event: You were transferred to a county transportation bureau as deputy director in charge of highway project bidding.

11110.event: You killed a drug lord on the border, were ambushed, died heroically, and received a posthumous first-class merit.

11111.event: You were selected for Army Special Ops, nicknamed 'Steel Spike', but died falling from a ten-meter cliff in training.

11112.event: On your first peacekeeping mission, you charged alone to rescue five engineers, was shot, and died heroically, earning the UN.

11113.event: Promoted to captain of a special operations squad, you led a counter-terrorism mission to the border.

11114.event: You died of a heart attack after late nights grading papers, before the ambulance came.

11115.event: Malnutrition and exhaustion caused your collapse; the landlord found you dead from severe depression with psychosis.

11116.event: A pandemic struck, masks returned, hospitals filled with coughs. You caught the virus and died.

11117.event: A cross-border conflict escalated into an airstrike, and you died when a bomb hit your apartment.

11118.event: A magnitude-7 earthquake hit the old district while you were asleep; you were crushed by falling debris and died.

11119.event: A chemical plant explosion created a toxic cloud that blotted out the sun; over the next three years, newborn deformity.

11120.event: The hottest summer on record; the asphalt softened and stuck to your shoes, and your old neighborhood had power rationing.

11122.event: While sensing legendary mana embers, a cold dark consciousness invaded your mind, devouring memories until you faded into darkness.

11123.event: You died in an accident. As your soul drifted, everything faded into eternal darkness.

11124.event: Defused sixteen mines, lost leg on seventeenth. Woke up, grinned at medal. Phantom limb pain on rainy days.

11125.event: Coughing, fever, difficulty breathing. Hospital crowded. You sat all night; shadows stood and fell. Epidemic killed you, clutching alcohol.

11126.event: In a jaundice phototherapy box, you saw blurry lights like fireflies; a nurse passed, and they vanished.

11127.event: At your first vaccination, a rainbow halo appeared and the pain eased.

11128.event: Found diary: on 31st birthday, red raincoat person changes life. Closed diary; it rained, red figure under streetlight.
11128.postEvent: You never approached them, but a figure appeared nightly at different corners until you moved.

11129.event: You watched a ghost hunt livestream, saw your hallway, heard your knock, then an eye watched you through the door.
11129.postEvent: The next day, you were found in bed, suffocated, with a thumbprint from a live-stream like on your face.

11130.event: Tree fell, watch inside with your name. Second hand matched heartbeat, stopped. You leaned on stump, never woke.
11130.postEvent: You remembered never burying anything, but the time on the watch matched your heartbeat exactly.

11131.event: At the bank, extra hell notes matched your dream; you walked to your deceased spouse on a golden road.
11131.postEvent: You pocket the spirit money, and the sun is blinding as you step out of the bank.

11132.event: At a flea market, you found a watch engraving counting down your age to zero, then darkness.

11134.event: The person ahead bought bubble wrap and popped it at the entrance while everyone ignored him.
11134.postEvent: You later discovered that the man came every Thursday to buy bubble wrap, rain or shine.

11135.event: You found a crushed lottery ticket worth $5, but it expired yesterday.
11135.postEvent: The clerk smiled and said, "Not even a minute late."

11136.event: Late night, kitchen chewing. Mouse with half cookie pushed it to you.
11136.postEvent: The next day, the cookie had disappeared without a trace.

11137.event: In a used bookstore, you find a coverless book; every page reads, 'Nice weather today, perfect for forgetting.'
11137.postEvent: You put down the book and went outside; the sky was overcast, threatening rain.

11138.event: A fortune teller stops you, says you were a tree for 300 years, then leaves without payment.
11138.postEvent: Later you found that there was no trace of any booth at that spot.

11139.event: Subway: old woman knitting. She got off, pulled needles, sweater turned to blue yarn. She walked away.

11140.event: You bought a fake plastic crystal online, but it revived a dying pothos plant.
11140.postEvent: You decided not to return it.

11141.event: A stray cat appeared every evening, and after you fed it, one morning it left a rusty coin and vanished.
11141.postEvent: You had it appraised as an antique coin, low monetary value but collectible.

11142.event: After working late, a clerk hands you a bento ordered by someone, though you're alone.
